name: composition
context: Compose multiple Terraform modules into complex infrastructure stacks with clean data flow and state boundaries
info: Module composition is the practice of combining multiple Terraform modules to build complete infrastructure. This skill covers composition patterns (flat, nested, wrapper), data flow between modules via outputs and variables, dependency management, module-to-module interface design, state boundary decisions, and Terragrunt for DRY multi-module orchestration. Real-world infrastructure is rarely a single module -- it is a composition of networking, compute, database, and security modules wired together. See [[terraform/modules]] for module fundamentals and [[terraform/modules/authoring]] for building composable modules.

sections.title: Composition patterns
sections.body: Three main patterns: (1) **Flat composition** -- root module calls multiple child modules side-by-side, wiring outputs to variables. Simple, transparent, recommended as the default. (2) **Nested modules** -- modules calling other modules internally for higher-level abstractions. Hides complexity but can make debugging harder. Use when there is a genuine abstraction boundary. (3) **Module wrapper pattern** -- thin wrapper around community or upstream modules that applies organizational defaults (tagging, naming, security). Enables standardization while leveraging maintained modules. Choose flat for most cases; nest sparingly; wrap to enforce policy.

sections.title: Data flow between modules
sections.body: Modules communicate through outputs and variables. The root module wires them: `module.a.output_name` feeds into `module.b`'s variable. This creates implicit dependencies that Terraform resolves automatically. For dependencies without data references, use `depends_on`. Design module outputs as stable APIs -- adding outputs is safe, removing them is a breaking change. Avoid `terraform_remote_state` inside modules; accept values as variables instead. See [[terraform/modules/authoring#best-practices]] for output design guidelines.

sections.title: State boundaries
sections.body: By default, all modules in a root configuration share one state file. Split into separate state files when: (1) different teams own different modules, (2) blast radius must be limited (networking vs application), (3) apply times are too long, (4) different change frequencies (VPC changes rarely, app config changes often). Use `terraform_remote_state` data source or Terragrunt `dependency` blocks to reference outputs across state boundaries. Separate state files add complexity -- only split when the benefits outweigh the cost. See [[terraform/state]] for state management details.

sections.title: Terragrunt orchestration
sections.body: Terragrunt is a wrapper around Terraform that provides: `dependency` blocks for cross-state module references, `include` for DRY root configuration, `run-all` for applying multiple modules in dependency order, auto-generated backend configurations, and environment-level variable files. Directory structure mirrors environments: `environments/<env>/<module>/terragrunt.hcl`. Each module gets its own state file. Terragrunt resolves the dependency graph and applies in the correct order. Alternative approaches include Terraform workspaces ([[terraform/workspaces]]) and CI/CD pipeline orchestration.

scripts.name: Scaffold Module Composition
scripts.description: Generate a root module that composes VPC, compute, and database modules with proper wiring
scripts.inline:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

# Scaffold a module composition with VPC + compute + database
# Usage: ENVIRONMENT=production STACK_DIR=./environments/production bash composition-scaffold.sh

ENVIRONMENT="${ENVIRONMENT:?ENVIRONMENT is required (e.g. dev, staging, production)}"
STACK_DIR="${STACK_DIR:-./environments/${ENVIRONMENT}}"
MODULES_DIR="${MODULES_DIR:-./modules}"

echo "Scaffolding composition for environment: ${ENVIRONMENT}"
echo "Stack directory: ${STACK_DIR}"

mkdir -p "${STACK_DIR}"

# main.tf
cat > "${STACK_DIR}/main.tf" << HCL
# ${ENVIRONMENT} environment composition
# Composes VPC + compute + database modules

locals {
  environment = "${ENVIRONMENT}"
  name_prefix = "${ENVIRONMENT}"
}

module "vpc" {
  source = "${MODULES_DIR}/vpc"

  name        = "\${local.name_prefix}-vpc"
  cidr        = var.vpc_cidr
  azs         = var.azs
  environment = local.environment
  tags        = var.tags
}

module "compute" {
  source = "${MODULES_DIR}/compute"

  name        = "\${local.name_prefix}-compute"
  vpc_id      = module.vpc.vpc_id
  subnet_ids  = module.vpc.private_subnet_ids
  environment = local.environment
  tags        = var.tags
}

module "database" {
  source = "${MODULES_DIR}/database"

  name        = "\${local.name_prefix}-db"
  vpc_id      = module.vpc.vpc_id
  subnet_ids  = module.vpc.database_subnet_ids
  environment = local.environment
  tags        = var.tags

  # Allow compute layer to connect
  allowed_security_group_ids = [module.compute.security_group_id]
}
HCL

# variables.tf
cat > "${STACK_DIR}/variables.tf" << 'HCL'
variable "vpc_cidr" {
  description = "CIDR block for the VPC"
  type        = string
  default     = "10.0.0.0/16"
}

variable "azs" {
  description = "Availability zones"
  type        = list(string)
}

variable "tags" {
  description = "Common tags for all resources"
  type        = map(string)
  default     = {}
}
HCL

# outputs.tf
cat > "${STACK_DIR}/outputs.tf" << 'HCL'
output "vpc_id" {
  description = "VPC ID"
  value       = module.vpc.vpc_id
}

output "compute_endpoint" {
  description = "Compute layer endpoint"
  value       = module.compute.endpoint
}

output "database_endpoint" {
  description = "Database endpoint"
  value       = module.database.endpoint
  sensitive   = true
}
HCL

# terraform.tfvars
cat > "${STACK_DIR}/terraform.tfvars" << HCL
vpc_cidr = "10.0.0.0/16"
azs      = ["us-east-1a", "us-east-1b", "us-east-1c"]

tags = {
  Environment = "${ENVIRONMENT}"
  ManagedBy   = "terraform"
}
HCL

# backend.tf
cat > "${STACK_DIR}/backend.tf" << HCL
terraform {
  backend "s3" {
    bucket         = "my-terraform-state"
    key            = "${ENVIRONMENT}/terraform.tfstate"
    region         = "us-east-1"
    dynamodb_table = "terraform-locks"
    encrypt        = true
  }
}
HCL

echo ""
echo "Composition scaffolded at ${STACK_DIR}/"
echo "Files created:"
find "${STACK_DIR}" -type f | sort | sed 's|^|  |'
echo ""
echo "Next steps:"
echo "  1. Ensure module directories exist in ${MODULES_DIR}/"
echo "  2. Update terraform.tfvars with your values"
echo "  3. Update backend.tf with your state bucket"
echo "  4. Run: cd ${STACK_DIR} && terraform init && terraform plan"

MODULES_DIR.description: Path to the shared modules directory

stackDir.description: Path to the scaffolded composition directory

depends.reason: Composable modules must be well-authored with clean interfaces
